Editor tool

Video Editor Portfolio Checklist

Check whether your portfolio gives hiring teams enough proof to understand your editing fit, speed, tools, rates, availability, and best work formats.

This tool supports editor-intent SEO and gives Reddit, Facebook, and forum replies a useful destination before asking editors to join the list.

Check

Make your editor profile easier to review

0% Needs proof
  • Portfolio link
  • Primary fit
  • Software
  • Experience level
  • Work preference
  • Rate range
  • Capacity
  • Turnaround
  • Availability
  • Proof notes

Profile preview

Add your details on the left and this preview will turn them into clearer editor intake notes.

See examples

The checklist saves only to this browser until you choose to submit the editor form.

Why editors get skipped

Hiring teams often move past portfolios that do not show the right format, pace, turnaround, workflow, or availability. The goal is not more clips. It is clearer proof for the work you want.

What to do after the score improves

Use the generated notes as a starting point, then join the editor list. The editor intake routes portfolio, fit, capacity, rate, and availability into the review queue.

Answers

Questions editors and hiring teams ask first

What should a video editor portfolio include?

A strong portfolio should include clips matched to the work you want, a clear primary editing fit, software and workflow details, rate or pricing context, availability, turnaround expectations, and notes about the formats you edit best.

Can I join the editor list before my portfolio is perfect?

Yes. The checklist helps you see what is missing, but editors can still join early if their portfolio, preferred formats, and availability are clear enough to review.

Does the checklist submit my editor profile automatically?

No. It creates a local draft in your browser. You choose whether to continue to the editor intake and submit your profile.
